items.headline: City launching business license amnesty program for shops
items.summary: Cupertino is offering an amnesty program to help shopping centers and businesses get their licenses in order, likely with reduced penalties or fees. This could affect local business compliance and tax revenue.

items.headline: New push to support retail and startup businesses with incentives
items.summary: The city is introducing economic development incentives specifically aimed at attracting and retaining retail stores and startups. This reflects a strategy to diversify Cupertino's economy beyond tech.

items.headline: New mixed-use housing project approved at 490 East Middlefield Road
items.summary: Mountain View approved a development agreement for a mixed-use residential project at 490 East Middlefield Road on the second reading. This project will add new housing and mixed-use space to the area.

items.headline: Mountain View applying for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for California's Prohousing Incentive Program (Round 4), which provides state funding to support housing development. If awarded, this could accelerate housing projects in Mountain View.

items.headline: City seeks public input on FY 2026-27 HUD housing grant spending plan
items.summary: Santa Clara is accepting comments on how it will use federal HUD housing grants for the upcoming fiscal year. This is your chance to weigh in on affordable housing priorities before the plan is finalized.

items.headline: City holds community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale is hosting a public meeting to discuss its safe parking initiative, which typically provides designated lots for people living in vehicles. This is a chance for residents to learn about or weigh in on how the city is addressing homelessness and vehicle dwelling.
